steps.title: Spot the key comparison
steps.content: The text compares Kleenex to “cellophane” using past tense: “as cellophane was.” This comparison implies cellophane underwent the same transformation Kleenex might experience—it went from being a specific product with trademark protection to becoming a generic term.

steps.title: Match the inference to the right answer
steps.content: Choice B captures this: cellophane “once referred to a specific product made by a particular company.” The other three don't fit: A invents debate not mentioned, C introduces irrelevant popularity, and D contradicts the facts (if something became generic, it must have been a trademark first). That matches choice B — B is correct.
